System Architecture

flowchart TD
  subgraph GroupA["Group A"]
    A1["Module A1"] --> A2["Module A2"]
  end
  subgraph GroupB["Group B"]
    B1["Module B1"] --> B2["Module B2"]
  end
  A2 --> B1

Category 1

moduleA

One-line description of module A

2-3 sentence summary of what this module does and why it matters.

~N filesNk LOCKey metric

moduleB

2-3 sentence summary.

~N filesNk LOC

Category 2

moduleC

A flat module (no sub-modules) — links to single page

Flat modules link to <module>/index.html instead of <module>_doc/index.html.

~N files

Navigation Guide

LevelWhat You'll FindWhen to Use
0 — Project Hub (this page)Project overview, module map, quick statsGetting started, finding the right module
1 — Module OverviewArchitecture diagram, key components, sub-module cardsUnderstanding a module's purpose and structure
2 — Sub-Module Deep DiveSource-level structs, algorithms, configs, diagramsImplementing, debugging, or extending code

How ModuleA & ModuleB Connect

Explanation of the boundary between the two major modules.

sequenceDiagram
    participant A as ModuleA Component
    participant Boundary as Protocol Boundary
    participant B as ModuleB Component
    A->>Boundary: Request (opcode)
    Note over A,Boundary: HIT → fast path done
    Boundary->>B: Forward on miss
    B-->>Boundary: Response data
    Boundary-->>A: Return to requester
Key insight: ModuleA owns X (components above the boundary). ModuleB owns Y (components below). The protocol at the boundary is Z — it uses credit-based flow control so neither side overwhelms the other.
